issues: News report was 11.8h stale and quoted $70,934 while live price was $68,145 — a $2,800 gap that could mislead downstream agents
Fundamentals report also 11.8h cached — hash rate spot dip flagged but never followed up on in the debate

Technical analyst self-contradicted on daily trend (labeled bearish/strong while reporting HH/HL + positive EMA slope) — the DATA QUALITY WARNING was appropriate but this should be caught upstream before it reaches debate agents
Bull analyst's self-awareness about past errors at $65,822 is good but risks anchoring — past failure shouldn't automatically discount current signals
Aggressive risk analyst made a compelling oversold case (1h RSI 27, 4h Stoch 0.48) that was appropriately overruled by event risk but deserved more structured counter-argument
improvements: News cache TTL of 12h is too long during active geopolitical events — consider an event-driven freshness override when war/crisis keywords are detected
Add a pre-debate consistency check that flags when an analyst labels a trend direction that contradicts its own reported price structure (HH/HL vs bearish label)
Risk Judge's 5/5 event risk score should auto-escalate to a hard HOLD constraint rather than being a soft input the FM can theoretically override — formalize the kill switch
Track the conditional triggers ($71,665 long / $67,000 short) in executor state so the next cycle can evaluate whether they were hit between runs
summary: High-quality decision cycle. The system correctly identified an environment where oversold technicals and shifting funding rates created a tempting long setup, but 5/5 event risk from active US-Iran conflict made any directional bet an unhedgeable gamble. The HOLD with defined re-entry triggers was the right call — the debate surfaced real tension between mean-reversion signals and fat-tail geopolitical risk, and the FM resolved it correctly by prioritizing capital preservation.
